title: rubus longiramulus
intro: Rubus longiramulus, also known as the Chinese thimbleberry, is a type of shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus longiramulus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, Rubus longiramulus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
